compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Confirmed the root cause + workaround: re-running review-plan on child 2711 (31 previously-indeterminate) with REBAR_LLM_MAX_TOKENS=64000 (up from DEFAULT_MAX_TOKENS=16000) let the verify step COMPLETE — verify_failed=None, indeterminate=0, and it returned a real BLOCK verdict with confirmed findings. This proves the truncation is the per-call output cap on the agentic verify step, and that scaling it (per the AC) resolves the INDETERMINATE degradation. 16000 is insufficient for ~30-finding container/cutover plans.

compiled_state.description:
## Summary

The plan-review gate's Pass-2 **agentic verify** step (`verify_branch → verify`, prompt
`plan-review-verifier-agentic`, `mode: structured`) truncates its output on finding-rich /
complex plans: the model hits the per-call **output** token cap (`finish_reason=length`) before
emitting the complete `plan_review_verification` structured result. The truncation aborts the
verify step, so every finding it was verifying degrades to **indeterminate** and the whole review
returns **verdict = INDETERMINATE** (`verify_failed=true`) instead of a real PASS/BLOCK — even when
the find pass produced legitimate, actionable findings.

## Reproduction Steps

1. Take a finding-rich plan — reproduced live on epic `6c9c-811f-6dc4-40e7` (7 ACs, multi-file) and
   task `2711-21bf-ab71-4136` (the reconciler cutover child). Both are in the store.
2. Run `rebar review-plan <id> --output json`.
3. Observe in the result:
   - `coverage.verify_failed = true`
   - `coverage.verify_error = "step 'review@then/verify_branch@then/verify' failed: the model hit
     the token cap before finishing (finish_reason=length) — its output is TRUNCATED; raise
     max_tokens or split the step"`
   - `verdict = INDETERMINATE`, `counts.indeterminate` large (e.g. 30–35), `counts.blocking = 0`.

Frequency: reproducible on complex plans (container epics, multi-file cutover tasks). Smaller plans
verify fine, so it scales with finding count / verification output size.

### Expected vs Actual

- **Expected:** the verify step completes and returns a full `plan_review_verification`, yielding a
  decisive PASS/BLOCK; a plan is only INDETERMINATE for genuine ambiguity, not because the verifier
  ran out of output budget.
- **Actual:** the verifier's structured output is cut off at the per-call `max_tokens`
  (`finish_reason=length`), the step fails, and the review collapses to INDETERMINATE with all
  candidate findings unverified.

## Root-cause pointers

- The error text is emitted by `src/rebar/llm/structured.py:193-194` (the `length` finish-reason
  guard) via the stop-reason check at `src/rebar/llm/runner.py:456`.
- The step is defined at `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/plan-review.yaml:130-146`. It already scales
  its **turn/tool-call** budget with `step_budget_per_item: 25` (from bug 59bc), but that governs
  agent turns, **not** the per-call output `max_tokens`.
- The per-call output cap is `cfg.max_tokens` → `model_settings["max_tokens"]`
  (`src/rebar/llm/runner.py:252-259`), defaulting to `DEFAULT_MAX_TOKENS`
  (`src/rebar/llm/config.py:266,471,535`). This is exactly the class of bug the find pass already
  hit and fixed — see the comment at `runner.py:254-255`: the default "4096 default — far too small
  for a multi-child container review, whose output truncated (stop_reason=max_tokens)". The verify
  step needs the analogous treatment: scale its output cap with finding count, or split the step.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The agentic verify step no longer truncates on finding-rich plans: re-running
      `rebar review-plan 6c9c-811f-6dc4-40e7` and `rebar review-plan 2711-21bf-ab71-4136` completes
      the verify step (`verify_failed` absent/false) and returns a decisive PASS/BLOCK.
- [ ] The verify step's per-call output `max_tokens` scales with the verification workload (e.g.
      derived from finding count / `step_budget_per_item`, mirroring the find-pass fix at
      `runner.py:252-259`) — or the step is split so each sub-call stays under the cap.
- [ ] When the output cap is genuinely hit despite scaling, the failure is surfaced distinctly
      (e.g. a dedicated "verifier truncated" signal) rather than silently degrading every finding to
      indeterminate, so operators can tell a truncation from real ambiguity.
- [ ] A regression test exercises a synthetic finding-rich verify input and asserts the step
      completes (no `finish_reason=length`) with the scaled cap.
- [ ] Non-integration suite green.
compiled_state.title: Plan-review agentic verify step truncates (finish_reason=length) on finding-rich plans -> whole review degrades to INDETERMINATE
